question: Will the US average gas price hit $5 per gallon in May 2026?
textDescription:
Resolution will be based on the official daily average gasoline price data provided at https://gasprices.aaa.com/. 
If any of the daily averages in May 2026 exceed $5.00, this market will resolve Yes. Otherwise, it will resolve No.
newsShortReason: US gas prices have exceeded $5 per gallon in May 2026 due to geopolitical tensions.
newsKeyEvidence: As of May 20, 2026, all 50 U.S. states have average gas prices above $4 per gallon, with seven states exceeding $5 per gallon. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/20/gas-prices-us-iran-war-4-dollars-50-states?utm_source=openai)) | The national average gas price reached $4.56 per gallon on May 20, 2026, a 53% increase since the conflict with Iran began. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/05/20/gas-prices-us-iran-war-4-dollars-50-states?utm_source=openai)) | Gas prices are expected to average $4.80 per gallon between Memorial Day and Labor Day, with potential to reach $5.02 if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/gas-prices-memorial-day-2026-iran-war/?utm_source=openai))
